title: Together, We CAN Affect the Ballot Initiative Votes
author: Nathan Weisser
orientation: A short October 2024 call-to-action urging abolitionists to stand outside Florida and Missouri polling places with abortion-imagery signs to defeat state ballot initiatives that would enshrine abortion in their constitutions. Aimed at Christian abolitionists who can mobilize on Election Day and at sympathetic readers wrestling with the post-Dobbs federalist landscape.
audiences: Christian abolitionists planning Election Day activism
Florida and Missouri voters facing abortion ballot initiatives
pro-life Christians confused about the post-Dobbs legal landscape
skeptics of using graphic abortion imagery in public witness
abolitionists debating states' rights versus federal duty

questions.question: What is Abolitionists Rising actually asking people to do at the Florida and Missouri polling places?
questions.audience: Christian abolitionists planning Election Day activism
questions.answer: They are asking abolitionists to legally stand outside polling places in Florida and Missouri while holding images of the abortions the ballot referendums would enshrine in those state constitutions. State-specific signs are available shipped from their store or as free downloadable PDFs that activists can print themselves.
questions.quote: In Florida and Missouri, we are asking abolitionists to stand outside of polling places (legally) while holding images of the abortions that these referendums are asking voters to enshrine in their state constitutions.

questions.question: Why focus the campaign on Florida and Missouri instead of every state with an abortion ballot measure?
questions.audience: Florida and Missouri voters facing abortion ballot initiatives
questions.answer: Weisser says Florida and Missouri were chosen for strategic reasons but encourages abolitionists in any other state to do the same work if they have the resources. General abortion-imagery signs are also offered for use outside the two focus states.
questions.quote: We are focusing on Florida and Missouri for strategic reasons, but obviously we’d encourage anyone in any of the other states to do the same if they have the resources.

questions.question: Why use graphic abortion imagery at all, given how disturbing it is?
questions.audience: skeptics of using graphic abortion imagery in public witness
questions.answer: Weisser argues the imagery is an effective tool that provokes one of three reactions: denial, anger at the exposer, or a seared conscience that can radically change a person's life. Many abolitionists themselves point to seeing such an image as the moment God turned them toward fighting abortion.
questions.quote: Abortion imagery is an awesome tool, and people tend to respond to it in different ways. Either they deny it is real, they just get mad that we’re exposing it, or the image is seared into their conscience, and they are never the same again.

questions.question: Did the fall of Roe v. Wade actually end abortion in states like Florida?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians confused about the post-Dobbs legal landscape
questions.answer: No. Weisser says many Christians, including many in Florida, mistakenly assumed the overturning of Roe ended abortion in their state, but that is far from reality. The Dobbs decision merely returned the question to the states rather than upholding federal protection for the preborn.
questions.quote: Most Christians thought that the fall of Roe meant the end of abortion in a lot of these states. Believe it not, even a lot of Floridian Christians thought abortion was gone in their state.

questions.question: How does the article characterize the Dobbs decision itself?
questions.audience: pro-life Christians confused about the post-Dobbs legal landscape
questions.answer: Weisser calls Dobbs a wicked Supreme Court decision because the justices refused to uphold the 14th Amendment and protect preborn life federally. Instead, he says, they washed their hands of the issue and handed the question down to the states, who have predictably moved swiftly toward evil.
questions.quote: Dobbs, the decision that overturned Roe, *was actually* a wicked supreme court decision, where they refused to uphold the 14th amendment and protect life federally.

questions.question: If the federal government has punted abortion to the states, what does the article think states should do?
questions.audience: abolitionists debating states' rights versus federal duty
questions.answer: Weisser argues that abdication by the federal government does not justify states doing the same. Just as abolitionists pre-Roe urged states to defy their federal head and abolish abortion without permission, states today should still break the cycle and refuse to enshrine the killing rather than meekly accepting the choice handed to them.
questions.quote: Just because the federal government has been abdicating its duty, doesn’t mean the states should, too.

questions.question: If these ballot amendments are defeated, would that abolish abortion?
questions.audience: Christian abolitionists planning Election Day activism
questions.answer: No. Weisser is candid that defeating the amendments would only stop abortion from being written into state constitutions; children would still be killed under existing state law. So victory would not really feel like a win, but it would suggest some remaining moral life in the country and reason for hope.
questions.quote: Keeping these amendments from passing would not abolish abortion. Not even close. All they would do is keep the legal nullity that is abortion from being enshrined into the state constitutions.

questions.question: What biblical framing does Weisser use to describe the polling-place witness?
questions.audience: Christian abolitionists planning Election Day activism
questions.answer: He frames the sidewalk sign-holding as confronting the culture with the Deuteronomy choice between life and death. Standing in front of voters with abortion imagery is, in his words, calling heaven and earth to witness against them and pleading with them to choose life so that they and their offspring may live.
questions.quote: We are essentially holding a sign in front of the culture, and saying to them, “I call heaven and earth to witness against you today, that I have set before you life and death, blessing and curse. Therefore choose life, that you and your offspring may live.”

related.title: How Shall an Abolitionist Vote?
related.reason: Companion 2024 piece on abolitionist voting ethics in the same election cycle this Florida/Missouri push targets.

related.title: Christians could force Trump to be an abolitionist of abortion with merely a lift of the finger. #YouDontGetMyVoteYET
